thought: I was just lucky. It wasn't actually my talent or hard work.

reading.title: Evidence Shield
reading.description: Examine facts. Luck doesn't coordinate launches, buuld things, or solve hard problems. What is one specific, hard achievement you worked for?
reading.placeholder: e.g., I researched for weeks to build the project, and spent 3 hours building the prototype.

writing.title: Self-Compassion Quill
writing.description: Acknowledge that you are allowed to learn. You don't have to know everything. What is one skill you've improved over the last year?
writing.placeholder: e.g., My writing style has gotten much clearer and faster since last year.

building.title: Action Hammer
building.description: Break the freeze state. Action kills self-doubt. What is one micro-task you can complete in the next 10 minutes?
building.placeholder: e.g., I can make a rough sketch for my art project.

painting.title: Curiosity Lens
painting.description: Shift focus from “Am I good enough?” to “What is interesting here?”. What is one curiosity you want to explore next?
painting.placeholder: e.g., I want to learn more about trees and how it can help us capture more carbon.

thought: They are going to realize I have no idea what I'm doing and find out I'm a fraud.

reading.title: Evidence Shield
reading.description: Who hired you? Experts. Believing you are a fraud means believing you tricked smart professionals. What is one piece of positive feedback you've received?
reading.placeholder: e.g., My peer said my documentation was super helpful last week.

writing.title: Self-Compassion Quill
writing.description: No one knows everything; everyone is figuring it out as they go. What is one area where you are still learning and that is completely okay?
writing.placeholder: e.g., I'm still learning advanced Gatsby routing, and that's a normal process.

building.title: Action Hammer
building.description: Doubt grows in isolation. What is one question you can ask a peer today to get unstuck?
building.placeholder: e.g., I will message Alex to ask for feedback on this layout structure.

painting.title: Curiosity Lens
painting.description: If you don't know something, it's just a new territory to explore. What topic in your current task looks the most fun to study?
painting.placeholder: e.g., Designing interactive SVG animations for this game widget.

thought: I must make no mistakes. If I fail at anything, it proves I'm incompetent.

reading.title: Evidence Shield
reading.description: Think of your mentors. Have they ever made mistakes? Yes. Mistakes are how experts are made. What is a past mistake you successfully recovered from?
reading.placeholder: e.g., I accidentally deleted a branch but restored it using git reflog.

writing.title: Self-Compassion Quill
writing.description: Dethrone perfectionism. You are a human, not a machine. What is a kind statement you would say to a friend who just made a minor error?
writing.placeholder: e.g., Don't worry, we'll patch it in the next hour. It's a quick fix.

building.title: Action Hammer
building.description: Action cures freeze. What is a small, low-stakes draft or sketch you can make right now?
building.placeholder: e.g., I will sketch the outline of this page on scrap paper without worrying about code.

painting.title: Curiosity Lens
painting.description: Mistakes are just feedback. What does a recent bug or design issue teach you about how this system works?
painting.placeholder: e.g., The build error showed me that path prefixes require leading slashes in Gatsby.

thought: I shouldn't ask for help. If I do, they will know I don't belong here.

reading.title: Evidence Shield
reading.description: Asking questions is a sign of high performance, not weakness. High performers gather data quickly. What is a time someone asked you for help and you respected them for it?
reading.placeholder: e.g., Sarah asked me about React hooks and it led to a great collaborative session.

writing.title: Self-Compassion Quill
writing.description: Remind yourself that collaboration is normal. We are social learners. Write a reframe accepting your need for collaboration.
writing.placeholder: e.g., Asking for help speeds up the project and builds team trust.

building.title: Action Hammer
building.description: Take the step. Write down a clear, specific question you need answered right now.
building.placeholder: e.g., Can you explain how the Gatsby Head API manages preconnecting fonts?

painting.title: Curiosity Lens
painting.description: Be curious about how others think. How can asking a teammate help you learn their problem-solving method?
painting.placeholder: e.g., I will observe how David debugs dependency audits so I can learn his terminal tricks.
